channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Bungie Confirms Marathon Will Have Seasonal Wipes Leaving Players ‘With Nothing’
item.content:encoded:
Bungie has expanded on Marathon’s seasonal structure, offering more information about the wipe model that will see player accounts reset every three months. This is a common enough operating model for extraction shooters, and it was popularized with Escape from Tarkov undergoing a wipe every six months or so.

In a new post on social media, Bungie has revealed that, every three months, Marathon will reset, leaving players ‘with nothing to their name’ and forcing them to build up their profile once again.

Marathon Wipes and Gives You a Clean Slate

In the recent article published on social media, Bungie confirmed that Marathon will have wipes, which was already known based on some interviews that surfaced in recent weeks. But now, the team has gone one step further, embellishing the information and letting us know exactly how wipes will work in Marathon.

Per the article:

Each season of Marathon brings a clean slate for the whole community with progression resets. These resets include everyone’s gear, contract progression, faction progression, and player level.

You’ll keep your achievements and ways to express yourself. This includes cosmetics (earned and paid), titles, and other rewards you earn by playing and reaching milestones each season. Your Codex progression also carries over, as unburying the secrets of Tau Ceti takes time.

It was also said that liaison contract progression will carry over, which means you won’t need to reconnect with the game’s factions once every three months.

This reset loop is to enable the game to ‘stay dangerous’, but it’s also a way to make loot feel more meaningful and enjoy a more balanced playing field without worrying about picking up the game down the line and being constantly outmatched by players.

Bungie has confirmed the names of the first two seasons, between which the first wipe will land:

  • Season 1: Death is the First Step
  • Season 2: Nightfall

Nightfall promises to introduce night-time raids to the game, pkunging Dire Marsh into darkness. It’ll also bring along a new Runner Shell, fresh weapons, mods, contracts, and more.

Are you looking forward to Marathon? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Tarkov’s PvE players have secured a big accolade

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

https://insider-gaming.com/bungie-confirms-marathon-wipes-leaving-players-nothing/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Marathon 2026 Roadmap—All Season 1 & Season 2 Content, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Bungie are preparing Marathon for the long haul, as the studio has unveiled its early plans for the extraction shooter. The roadmap reveals content plans that lead all the way to November, so the first six months are seemingly all planned ahead.

Seasons are set to last for three months apiece in Marathon, so there’s plenty of time to unpack the content Bungie packs into each one. That means that Season 1 and Season 2 are seemingly prepared, and fans can already look ahead to what is in store for them.

If you’re interested in seeing what the future of Marathon looks like, then you’re in the right place, as Insider Gaming has it all broken down below.

Marathon Season 1 2026 Roadmap Content, Explained—March To June

Marathon kicks things off with Season 1: DEATH IS THE FIRST STEP, which launches alongside the game’s official launch on March 5, and there’s a lot of content for you to unpack.

It’s perhaps the most important Season of Marathon’s life cycle, as the game needs to launch with substantial content to keep players hooked and invested in the longevity of the game. Thankfully, it seems that Bungie has a lot in store, and all of the launch content is listed for you down below:

  • Complete Faction and Contract progression
  • All 6 Factions, each with its own Upgrades and progression
  • All 6 launch Runner Shells
  • 3 Zones (Outpost will unlock the day after launch)
  • 28 Weapons, including Mods, Implants, Cores, and more
  • Earnable Cosmetics, Achievements, and Storytelling through the Codex

Whilst that seems like a sizable chunk of content to work through, there’s still more content planned to drop throughout Season 1 of Marathon. Most notably, the fourth zone, Cryo Archive, will unlock once the community unlocks its way into orbit. Expect this to arrive mid-March, alongside Ranked mode, which Bungie will share more details about soon.

  • More Insider Gaming: Factions In Marathon, Explained

Here’s everything else that you can expect to arrive in Marathon throughout Season 1:

  • Cryo Archive Zone
  • Ranked Mode
  • C.A.R.R.I. Event
  • New Gun
  • Mid-Season Tuning
  • New Implants

Marathon Season 2 2026 Roadmap Content, Explained—June To August

Following Season 1 of Marathon is Season 2: NIGHTFALL, where you can expect new Zone conditions, as well as new systems that will change the way you play the game.

  • More Insider Gaming: Every Runner Shell In Marathon, Explained

Bungie confirmed that each new Season will have its own unique theme and combination of features to continuously evolve the world and gameplay of Marathon. It’s a promising notion, though details are admittedly more scarce for Season 2. Here’s what’s known so far:

  • Night Marsh, Dire Marsh Zone Condition
  • New Runner Shell
  • New System, The Cradle
  • New Guns
  • New Mods
  • New Threats
  • And More

Marathon Season 3 2026 Roadmap Content, Explained—August To November

The current Marathon roadmap doesn’t reveal any information for Season 3 of Marathon, other than the fact that it will start in August, and end in November.

There’s little need to see that far into the future at this stage in the game’s lifecycle, so it’s not a problem that Season 3 remains a mystery. So long as Bungie are confident in their 2026 roadmap, that should be enough for the fans.

Are you excited for all the planned content coming to Marathon? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Marathon content, check out Bungie’s plans to counter cheaters, as well as whether Marathon has crossplay.
